profile.name: Pardhu Sri Rushi Varma Konduru
profile.title: Independent ML Researcher in Reversible Neural Adaptation and Statistical Machine Learning
profile.bio: Exploring statistical machine learning, autonomous systems, and runtime-governed learning architectures. Building modular, interpretable, and compositionally safe intelligence systems with a focus on behavioral robustness, reversibility, adaptation, and stability in autonomous driving and adaptive world models.

about.title: Research Philosophy
paragraphs: I am an independent researcher and final-year cybersecurity undergrad working at the intersection of machine learning, AI security, and adaptive systems.

My research focuses on how machine learning systems behave under long-term adaptation, distributional shifts, and changing optimization dynamics. I study the statistical and structural properties of adaptive learning systems, investigating how architectural and training design choices influence robustness, generalization, controllability, and behavioral recoverability.

My recent work introduces Reversible Neural Adaptation (RLAE), a structural paradigm that separates behavioral learning from model identity, enabling deterministic rollback and addressing irreversible behavioral drift in weight-based adaptation. This work formalizes concepts such as structural irreversibility, recoverability, and behavioral divergence, supported by empirical evaluation across adaptation regimes and model scales.

In parallel, I am developing a strong foundation in statistical machine learning and applied mathematics—particularly probability theory, optimization, and information theory—and applying these concepts to my research on latent behavioral structures in low-dimensional statistical manifolds for autonomous driving systems. My work explores how learning dynamics, manifold geometry, and distributional shifts shape behavioral adaptation, stability, interpretability, and robustness in autonomous world models and decision-making driving agents.

My research interests include statistical and robust machine learning, adversarial machine learning, structural robustness of adaptive models, AI safety and controllability, and runtime-governed modular learning systems.
My goal is to contribute to the design of trustworthy machine learning systems that remain stable, interpretable, and controllable under continuous adaptation.

researchAreas.title: Reversible Behavioral Learning
researchAreas.subtitle: Structural Identity Preservation
researchAreas.description: Reversible Behavioral Learning (RBL) is a structurally separated adaptation paradigm where adaptive behaviors are encoded in removable behavioral parameterizations while the core identity parameters remain frozen, enabling deterministic rollback and exact recoverability through explicit behavioral unloading.

researchAreas.title: Latent Behavioral State Machines
researchAreas.subtitle: Statistical Learning & Behavioral Manifolds
researchAreas.description: Investigating the manifold geometry of adaptive agent telemetry through statistical learning, latent state-space modeling, regime separability, and behavioral dynamics in evolving autonomous systems.
researchAreas.keywords: latent manifolds • statistical learning • regime separability • behavioral telemetry

publications.title: On the Structural Limitations of Weight-Based Neural Adaptation and the Role of Reversible Behavioral Learning
publications.metadata: Preprint • arXiv: arXiv:2603.02934 • DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18761938, 10.48550/arXiv.2603.02934 • 2026 • AI Safety / Machine Learning
publications.abstract: Modern neural models are typically adapted by updating parameters through fine-tuning or alignment. While effective for task performance, such updates can degrade reasoning, overwrite capabilities, or alter functional identity. We examine this as structural irreversibility and propose reversible adaptation frameworks that isolate learned behaviors from core parameters, enabling deterministic rollback with measurable recoverability guarantees.

systems.name: RLAE (REVA4)
systems.description: Runtime-Governed Intelligence Systems for modular, reversible, and compositionally safe behaviors. Enables dynamic loading of behavioral LoRA patches with deterministic rollback capabilities. Implements behavioral governance and runtime safety constraints for adversarial resilience.

systems.name: LBSM
systems.description: Latent Behavioral State Machines: A statistical learning and behavioral manifold research framework for modeling adaptive agent telemetry. Explores latent state-space geometry, regime separability, behavioral transitions, and manifold dynamics in evolving autonomous systems through dimensionality reduction, clustering, and telemetry-driven analysis.

systems.name: Project Ouroboros
systems.description: Modular ESP32-based cybersecurity platform for wireless defense, monitoring, and controlled research. Hardware and firmware modules for cyber-physical security experimentation, featuring embedded systems integration and real-time RF threat response capabilities.

contact.collaborationText: Open to research collaboration and academic discussion. Focused on contributing to machine learning research and advancing the field through robust, trustworthy, and adaptive systems.
contact.notes: Perhaps we are only vessels of a greater curiosity
contact.footerNote: Available for research collaboration and academic partnerships.
